events.event_description: Legation established when John Adams presented credentials as Minister Plenipotentiary to the Netherlands.

evidence.quote: John Adams, Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1781-01-01. Credentials presented: 1782-04-19.

events.event_description: Legation effectively closed when William Vans Murray presented his recall; no successor was appointed until 1815, leaving a ~14-year gap in representation.

evidence.quote:
William Vans Murray, Minister Resident. Appointed: 1797-03-02. Credentials presented: 1797-06-20. Presented recall on 1801-09-02. Note: Commissioned to the Batavian Republic.

William Eustis, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1814-12-19. Credentials presented: 1815-07-20.

events.event_description: Legation re-established when William Eustis presented credentials as Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary, ending a ~14-year gap in representation.

evidence.quote: William Eustis, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1814-12-19. Credentials presented: 1815-07-20. Had farewell audience 1818-05-05.

events.event_description: Legation in The Hague closed by Gordon following German occupation of the Netherlands.

evidence.quote: In anticipation of German occupation, the Government left the Netherlands on 1940-05-13. Note: Gordon closed the Legation on July 15, 1940, and left post on July 16, 1940.

events.event_description: Legation re-established near the Dutch Government-in-exile in the United Kingdom, with Rudolf Schoenfeld making his initial call as Chargé d'Affaires ad interim.

evidence.quote: Rudolf E. Schoenfeld established the Legation near the Government of the Netherlands established in the United Kingdom, making his initial call as Chargé d'Affaires ad interim on Aug 15, 1940.

events.event_description: Mission elevated from Legation to Embassy when Biddle was promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary, while still serving near the Dutch Government-in-exile in London.

evidence.quote: Anthony Joseph Drexel Jr Biddle, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1941-02-11. Credentials presented: 1941-03-27. Promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary 1942-05-08.
